summary: GKKS Vivah Step 6 — Browse Profiles. Backend: 21/21 tests passed (100%). Frontend: All 12 critical flows verified. Core browse functionality works correctly — 8 profiles load, all 4 filter types functional. ONE CRITICAL UI BUG found: Search button (for Age filter) is completely off-screen at x=639px on 375px mobile viewport. Age filter cannot be triggered via UI tap. Metro bundler cache served stale bundle (Browse Profiles button missing) until Expo was restarted — fixed by supervisor restart. MOCKED: MSG91 WhatsApp OTP, Cloudflare Turnstile (WEB_BYPASS).

ui_bugs.issue: CRITICAL: Search button is rendered off-screen (x=639px on 375px viewport). The filter row has 4 items: City(flex:2), Age min(flex:1), Age max(flex:1), Search button(no flex). On Expo Web, React Native flex maps to CSS flex which uses 'flex: 1 1 auto' (auto basis), causing City/Age inputs to overflow and push Search button off-screen. Age range filter CANNOT be applied by tapping Search on web. City filter works via Enter key (onSubmitEditing). Age filter accessible via JS but not via UI.
ui_bugs.testID: N/A — no testID on Search button or filter inputs

ui_bugs.issue: During initial testing, Browse Profiles button was missing from home screen because Metro bundler served a cached bundle from before Step 6 changes. Fixed by restarting Expo via 'sudo supervisorctl restart expo'. This forced a fresh 842-module bundle. Not a code bug but a deployment/cache awareness issue.

issues: Filter row layout overflow on mobile web: City + Age min + Age max + Search button do not fit in 343px (375px - 32px padding). Search button at x=639px is 264px off-screen. Fix: use two rows or flex-wrap, OR add 'flex: 0, minWidth: 60' to Search button, OR replace with icon button. Note: on native React Native, flex:N uses flexBasis:0 which would fit correctly — this is a web-specific rendering difference.

Missing testID attributes on all filter components: city input, age min input, age max input, search button, gender pills, profile cards, back button, count text. Makes automated testing rely on placeholder text and text content selectors only.

react_native_issues.issue: In React Native Web, flex shorthand maps to CSS 'flex: N 1 auto' (not 'flex: N 1 0px' like native). This causes flex children to use their natural content width as basis, potentially overflowing the container. City input (flex:2) with text + padding may grow larger than expected, pushing Search button off-screen. Age min/max inputs with flex:1 and no minWidth defined.

action_items: CRITICAL: Fix Search button off-screen issue in browse.tsx filter row. Options: (a) Use two rows — gender pills row + City input row + separate age row with Search button, (b) Add 'flex: 0, minWidth: 60' to searchBtn style, (c) Replace text 'Search' button with icon. Root cause: React Native Web renders flex differently from native — use flexShrink:0 on non-flex children.
LOW: Add testID attributes to browse.tsx interactive elements: city-input, age-min-input, age-max-input, search-button, all-pill, male-pill, female-pill, profile-card-{id}, count-text, back-button.
LOW: Run 'sudo supervisorctl restart expo' after bundle changes to ensure fresh Metro bundle is served.
critical_code_review_comments: browse.tsx filter row: flexDirection:'row' with 4 items (flex:2 + flex:1 + flex:1 + no-flex Search) overflows on web. React Native Web uses CSS flex with auto basis vs native's 0 basis. Fix: either wrap to two rows or explicitly set width/flex on the Search button.

browse.tsx: fetchProfiles is called on mount with fetchProfiles('All') — correctly loads all 8 profiles. Gender pills call fetchProfiles(g) directly. But Search button (which handles city + age range) is unreachable on mobile web viewport.

browse.tsx: age inputs (Age min, Age max) have no onSubmitEditing handler. Users cannot apply age filter via keyboard on these inputs. Only way is via Search button (which is off-screen on web). Recommend adding onSubmitEditing to age inputs or using a different UX pattern.
browse.tsx: No testID attributes on any interactive elements. Full testing requires text-based selectors or JS evaluation.
server.py browse_profiles: Queries status IN ('pending_approval', 'claimed') — correctly excludes 'unclaimed'. All 8 seeded profiles are 'pending_approval', confirming they show in browse.
server.py browse_profiles: Age calculation is correct — uses (today.month, today.day) < (dob.month, dob.day) for birthday-aware age calculation.

success_rate.frontend: 85% — All 12 core browse flows verified. Search button unreachable on web (x=639px off-screen) prevents tapping age filter. Workaround: JS click or city Enter key. Gender pills, city Enter, back button all work correctly.

context_for_next_testing_agent: Step 6 Browse Profiles tested. Backend 100% pass. Frontend browse screen works. CRITICAL: Search button off-screen (x=639 on 375px). Age filter inaccessible via UI. Use phone +919876543211 (candidate, no profile) for testing. OTP from: grep 'OTP for +919876543211' /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log | tail -1. After any code changes, restart Expo: sudo supervisorctl restart expo. All 8 profiles are pending_approval in Supabase — do NOT change their status.
rca_of_issue: ROOT CAUSE of Search button off-screen: browse.tsx filter row uses flexDirection:'row' with: City(flex:2), Age_min(flex:1), Age_max(flex:1), Search(no flex). In React Native native, flex:N means flexGrow:N, flexShrink:1, flexBasis:0. In React Native Web (CSS), flex:N maps to flexGrow:N, flexShrink:1, flexBasis:auto. With auto basis, City input takes its content width FIRST (~80-100px minimum), then grows. This causes the row total to exceed container width, pushing the Search button past x=375px. MITIGATION: (1) Add flex:0 to searchBtn OR (2) Restructure the filter row into separate lines OR (3) Add flexShrink:0 to searchBtn so it's not compressed off-screen. REPRODUCTION: Load browse screen in browser at 375px viewport, inspect Search button getBoundingClientRect().x — will be ~639px.
